title: AI Inventory Forecasting Tools: A Practical Guide from Excel to Machine Learning to Eliminate Stockouts and Overstock
excerpt: How cross-border e-commerce solo operators can use AI for inventory forecasting — comparing Inventory Planner, Prediko, Cogsy, and building a complete system.
content:
## Inventory Management Is E-Commerce's Biggest Hidden Cost

Every e-commerce seller knows the nightmare: a product goes viral and you are out of stock. By the time restock arrives, the hype is gone. Or the reverse — you stock up for 3 months, nothing moves, and your capital is frozen in a warehouse.

Solo operators have tighter capital and less margin for error. One bad inventory decision can wipe out a month's profit.

Traditional methods rely on intuition and spreadsheets. AI inventory forecasting tools transform this from guesswork to data-driven decision-making.

## Why Traditional Methods No Longer Cut It

Excel is not unusable — it is just not good enough. It cannot handle seasonal fluctuations, promotional impact, supply chain delays, or competitor actions. AI inventory forecasting incorporates all these variables into a model.

## Choose the Right Tool

**Inventory Planner** is the most full-featured tool with AI demand forecasting, auto-replenishment suggestions, multi-warehouse management, promotion simulation, and category analysis. Starts around $79/month for stores under 500 SKUs.

**Prediko** focuses on the Shopify ecosystem with fast onboarding. Features include one-click Shopify connection, AI demand forecasting using 90-day trends, low-stock alerts, raw materials management with BOM, and auto-generated purchase orders. Starter at $25/month.

**Cogsy** offers end-to-end replenishment operations management including purchase orders, inbound tracking, and supplier management starting around $99/month.

**Free Alternative**: Export 12 months of sales to Google Sheets, use FORECAST.ETS for seasonal forecasting, paste into ChatGPT for analysis recommendations.

## Data Foundation

Clean your sales data: exclude returns and canceled orders, exclude B2B bulk orders, flag promotional periods, and record stockout periods. Safety stock formula: Z-score x standard deviation of demand x sqrt(replenishment lead time).

## ABC Classification

A-items (20% of SKUs, 80% revenue): daily monitoring, 99% service level, auto-replenishment. B-items (30% of SKUs, 15% revenue): weekly monitoring, 95% service level, manual review. C-items (50% of SKUs, 5% revenue): monthly check, low safety stock, buy on demand.

## Exception Handling

Human review needed for new product launches (first 8 weeks), sudden social media virality, supply chain disruptions, major competitor actions, and seasonal transitions. Set alerts for stock below safety stock, forecast changes over 50%, and delivery delays over 3 days.

## FAQ

**Q: Does a small store under 100 orders/month need this?** A: Prediko at $25/month or a free Google Sheets solution is sufficient. AI forecasting advantages become clear above 300 orders/month.

**Q: How to forecast seasonal products?** A: Need 12 months of historical data minimum. Year one relies on manual judgment. Year two onward AI seasonal forecasting is accurate.

**Q: Managing inventory across multiple platforms?** A: Use Inventory Planner for multi-platform support or create a consolidated Google Sheets dashboard.

**Q: How much safety stock?** A: FMCG: 7-14 days. Fashion: 14-21 days. Electronics: 3-7 days. Custom products: zero-stock model.

## Conclusion

Inventory management is the silent profit killer in e-commerce. Start with the simplest option and run it for 2-3 months. Upgrade gradually. Do not wait for a stockout to wish you had started forecasting.
